name: Out-of-Band Exfiltration
description: No data is reflected and time-based techniques are blocked. Use out-of-band (OAST) techniques to exfiltrate the flag via DNS or HTTP requests from the database server to a webhook you control.
briefing: ArcNet's inventory system has an endpoint vulnerable to SQL injection, but all in-band data channels are closed: no data is reflected, errors are suppressed, and the query has a forced timeout of 1 second (killing time-based approaches). However, the MySQL server can make outbound connections. Use out-of-band techniques — such as LOAD_FILE() with a UNC/URL path or DNS exfiltration via CONCAT with a domain lookup — to send the flag from the 'oast_secrets' table to a webhook endpoint you control. The lab includes a built-in webhook catcher at /webhook for convenience.
objective: Use out-of-band SQL injection techniques to exfiltrate the flag to the built-in webhook catcher at /webhook.
author: h4ck0rman
